BUG_1_WORD_ORDER.description: Word Order game now correctly compares sentences by stripping trailing punctuation
BUG_1_WORD_ORDER.evidence: Code fix in /app/frontend/src/components/games/grammar/WordOrder.js line 63: normalize function uses regex /[.!?,;:]+$/g to strip trailing punctuation before comparison
BUG_1_WORD_ORDER.before: 'It is a kite' vs 'It is a kite.' would show 'Not quite!' (false negative)
BUG_1_WORD_ORDER.after: Both compare as 'it is a kite' after normalization - shows 'Perfect!'

BUG_2_LISTENING_OPTIONS.description: Listening questions now have proper options instead of defaulting to Yes/No
BUG_2_LISTENING_OPTIONS.evidence: API returns options like ['two', 'three', 'four'] for 'How many lions?' questions
